calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-21-2009 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Issue Regarding Surcharge
Off Calendar Dec-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for David White.

Mr. Jones appeared for the purpose of addressing the surcharge issue. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-30-2009 Continued To Dec-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for David White.

Mr. Jones appeared and requested that the Court call this matter. Mr. Jones stated that he had a conflict with the December 14, 2009 date. At the request of Mr. Jones, the Court continued the matter to December 21, 2009.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-16-2009 Continued To Nov-30-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for and with David White.

The Court stated that receipts from the two creditors were needed or alternativley, some proof from the bank showing that the cashiers checks had cleared. The Court also stated that sanctions have stopped running as of November 10, 2009. The matter was continued to November 30, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. White's appearance is excused on that date.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-05-2009 Continued To Nov-16-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for David White.

Mr. Jones stated that the sanctions were paid over the weekend, that the petition for final distribution was to be filed in a couple of days, that he misunderstood this Court's order, and that copies of what he had paid will be provided to the Court. The Court inquired whether counsel had proof of what he had received or paid out with him today. Counsel stated no. The Court inquired why he did not bring proof with him today. Mr. Jones again stated that he misunderstood the Court's order, that he can have the proof delivered to the Court tomorrow, that he purchased money orders at Citibank in Milbrae, and that they were mailed out over the weekend. In addition, counsel stated that he had spoken with someone at the bonding company and that he had received a bill from them. The Court inquired as to the creditor's claim. Mr. Jones stated that he had reviewed the code, that there was no interest due, and that he paid the "face" amount. In addition, Mr. Jones stated as to the sanctions that he believed they should only be imposed for "court" days not "calendar" days. The Court noted that counsel had mailed out payments over the weekend, and then set status on the proof of payment of the creditor's claim and the bond premium for for November 16, 2009; ordering counsel to serve the creditor and the surety company. Mr. Jones inquired whether the Court would sign the order. The Court stated it would sign the order on the 16th, ordered counsel to send notice to the creditor and surety, ordered that he provide the Court with the surety's bill and creditor's claim, and submit proof of payment along with a proposed order by the close of business on November 10, 2009. The Court noted that sanctions would be discussed on November 16, 2009.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-14-2009 Continued To Oct-05-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for and with David White.

Mr. Jones requested another continuance, stated that the sanctions would be paid and that the estate would be closed. In addition, he stated that the ambulance bill was $800.00, that the bond premium was $2,850.00 and that he was unable to pay them at this time. The Court stated that sanctions effective today were $50.00 per day. The matter was continued to October 5, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith./J. Shannon.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-10-2009 Continued To Sep-14-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for and with David White.

Mr. Jones stated that he expected to have the requested documents filed very shortly. The Court informed Mr. Jones that sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day would begin today. The matter was continued to September 14, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the Amended Petition For Final Disrtibution, for proof of payment of the Creditor's Claim, for proof of payment of the bond premium and for payment of sanctions. Sanctions will stop when the proof of payment of the Creditor's Claim and the bond premium are filed.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Wendy Pun, CSR#12891. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-23-2009 Continued To Aug-10-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for and with David White (Administrator).

Mr. Jones stated that the creditor's claim and the bond will be paid within 30 days as he anticipates having access to the funds in England at that time. The Court continued the matter to August 10, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and gave Mr. Jones the following options: 1)Cure the defects on the Petition For Final Distribution. 2)Amend the petition to close the estate for lack of assets. 3)Pay the creditor's claim and the bond premium, and submit the order to close the estate. If the estate is not in a position to be closed by the continued date, sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day against Counsel John Jones will be imposed until the estate is closed.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-21-2009 Continued To Jun-23-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for and with David White.

Mr. Jones appeared for the purpose of requesting a 30 day continuance. The matter was continued to June 23, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on release of funds from the U. K. or status on the petition to close the estate.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-18-2009 Continued To Apr-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for David White.

Mr. Jones reported that the gathering of the assets could be done within 30 to 60 days. The Court continued the matter to April 21, 2009.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-11-2009 Continued To Feb-18-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-27-2009 Continued To Feb-11-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for David White.

Mr. Jones provided copies of the signed verifications dated October 14, 2008 to the Court and stated that they were not filed due to an oversight on his part. In addition, he stated that the ambulance bill would be paid as soon as they received the money from England, that he was in contact with the Attorney in England and requested a two week continuance. Furthermore, he stated that he did not know when the funds would be released. The Court stated that this information should have been provided today and questioned the next course of action. The Court suggested that if this information was not received, that he work out an agreement with the creditor and the bond agency for the Client to pay the bills and close the estate due to lack of funds. The matter was continued to February 11, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the funds and on the efforts made for resolving the debts.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Shannon.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-30-2008 Continued To Jan-27-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for David White (Petitioner).

Mr. Jones stated that Mr. White was located in San Francisco and that his office made an error in not having the petition verified. In addition, he stated that the ambulance bill had not been paid as Mr. White had no personal funds to pay the bill, that the bond premiums had not been paid but that he was in communication with the bond agency and that these items would be paid upon receipt of the $50,000.00 preliminary distribution and/or receipt of the estate funds which were both in England. The matter was continued to January 27, 2009 for David White to appear, for the Petition For Final Distribution to be verified and for Mr. White to address the unpaid ambulance bill as well as the unpaid bond premiums.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Leticia Perez, CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-19-2008 Continued To Dec-30-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for David White.

Mr. Jones requested a one month continuance. The matter was continued to December 30, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Guitron.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-15-2008 Continued To Nov-19-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for David White.

Mr. Jones stated that he had contact with the Executor, that they had not received the funds from England to pay the creditors claims and requested a 30 day continuance. In addition, he stated that he needed an order to transfer the assets from the decedent to the Executor, that he was in touch with English Counsel and the bond agency's attorney, and that there were no funds in the United States. The matter was continued to November 19, 2008.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Wong.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-17-2008 Continued To Oct-15-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-03-2008 Continued To Sep-17-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-20-2008 Continued To Sep-03-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Filing The Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-01-2008 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for David White (Executor).

Mr. Jones stated that he filed the Petition For Final Distribution and that the hearing date was set for August 20, 2008. The matter was taken off calendar.

Judge: Raymond Arata. Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto CSR#5229. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Filing The Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-10-2008 Continued To Jul-01-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for David White (Administrator).

Mr. Jones stated that he met with his client on Sunday and that they were working on the Petition For Final Distribution. The matter was continued to July 1, 2008.

Judge John Dearman. Reporter: Tamara Willat-Harrison CSR#4609. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Filing The Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-06-2008 Continued To Jun-10-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for David White.

Mr. Jones requested a continuance stating that he needed more time to complete the accounting as he had relocated his office. The Court informed Mr. Jones that the fees for the personal representative and Counsel would probably be reduced due to the matter being delayed. The matter was continued to June 10, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Filing The Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-01-2008 Continued To May-06-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES- ATTY. JOHN EDMUND JONES FOR THE PETITIONER DAVID E. WHITE.

MR. JONES ASKED THE COURT TO CONTINUE THE MATTER TO GIVE HIM MORE TIME TO PREPARE THE PETITION FOR FINAL DISTRIBUTION. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MATTER TO MAY 6, 2008 AND NO APPEARANCE IS REQUIRED IF THE PETITION HAVE BEEN FILED PRIOR TO THE HEARING DATE.

Report Of Status Of Estate And Petition For Preliminary Distribution

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jones for David White (Petitioner).

Mr. Jones stated that he needed time to give proper notice. The matter was continued to March 4, 2008 for status. Counsel is expected to file the Petition for Final Distribution by that date.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Obi.
